title: Regression or issue persistence: "command not found: podman" after install on m1 mac
body:
### Issue Description

"command not found: podman" after install on m1 mac, already reported in #15542. OP there closed it without clear resolution & issue persists.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

On an m1 mac (MPB 16" 2021 running Ventura 13.3.1):

Install podman desktop, then follow the "Podman is not installed on this system, would you like to install Podman 4.5.0?" prompt. Complete the installation wizard; select to install for all users on this machine.

Go to terminal and type `podman info`; type `rehash` and try again



### Describe the results you received

```
zsh: command not found: podman
```


### Describe the results you expected

Successful run of the command

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

M1 mac
Physical


### Additional information

This was already reported in #15542. OP there closed it without clear resolution.

Podman command is indeed installed at: `/opt/podman/bin` but this is not in path. No update by installer to `~/.zshenv`, `~/.zshrc` or `~/.zprofile`. In fact, doing `grep podman ~/.*` shows nothing except for the `~/.zsh_history` entries.

I'm actually following [this Red Hat guide here](https://developers.redhat.com/articles/2023/02/14/remote-container-development-vs-code-and-podman#install_podman_desktop_on_the_local_machine), and if I simulate what it prescribes:

```
/opt/podman/bin/podman version
```

I get:

```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v4.5.0/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///var/folders/61/pb7hvq8x34jc9v6cc017kx2r0000gn/T/podman-run--1/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```

Running 

```
/opt/podman/bin/podman machine init
/opt/podman/bin/podman machine start
```

Fixes it, as expected, but those steps are not in the above guide. Resulting version output:

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.5.0
API Version:  4.5.0
Go Version:   go1.19
Git Commit:   75e3c12579d391b81d871fd1cded6cf0d043550a
Built:        Fri Apr 14 10:04:39 2023
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.5.0
API Version:  4.5.0
Go Version:   go1.20.2
Built:        Fri Apr 14 09:41:12 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

### SIDE NOTE:

This link in the bug guidance template is wrong: https://github.com/containers/podman/README.md. It should be https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/README.md

comments.body: Did you restart your shell after the install?

That was the purpose of running `rehash`. I should not have to restart the shell. Furthermore, as I mentioned, I checked all my dotfiles, and they were not updated, so I don't understand how restarting the shell would have done anything.

Above comment triggered a thought: OP in #15542 mentioned an update to `.bash_profile`. But Macs have switched to zsh by default for a while now. I do not have bash, nor any of its dotfiles, and certainly not `.bash_profile`. Could the issue be that the podman installer isn't set up for the default zsh-only?
We do not touch any dot file, the installer writes the PATH to /etc/paths.d/podman-pkg. I would expect that to be picked up by your shell on a restart. I do not own a mac so I cannot test it.

OK I did launch a new shell, and that works. `/opt/podman/bin` is in the `PATH`. So you were right, I needed to restart the shell, and running `rehash` was not enough, but that's pretty annoying. I guess it's more an issue with the Mac OS setup than with podman, though, so I'll close this. I just recommend making sure your various guides are updated to b clear on this point, because old-school UNIX/Linux heads like me are not going to be thinking they should need to restart their shells without very good reason.

I'm also glad we have this ticket with a little more context for the next person who comes along.
I guess running the command here should be enough: https://apple.stackexchange.com/a/128903
Tested. ```eval `/usr/libexec/path_helper -s` ``` did the trick. Guess I learned something useful today. Thanks!
